themes.name: Mideast Conflict Energy Shock
themes.description: Escalation in the Middle East is driving Brent crude prices up, testing central bank inflation control and leading to thematic investments in energy security and infrastructure.

themes.description: Hyperscalers' capex is hitting $131B in Q1, driving electrification and infrastructure buildout amid the Mideast energy shock.

themes.name: Broad S&P Earnings Beats
themes.description: 74% of companies are beating EPS expectations, with median stock growth at 11%, the highest in five years.

themes.name: Medicaid Managed Care Turnaround
themes.description: Analyst upgrades after Medicaid margins bottom in 2026, with enrollment down and risk pool stabilizing.

themes.name: Tokenized Credit & Crypto Yield Plays
themes.description: Shift in demand from crypto to tokenized credit products for yield, with new launches like US commercial paper on Solana.

themes.description: Rotation from max bullish Mag 7 to caution, with tech leading earnings but broader market jitters due to conflict and volatility.

themes.description: Despite earnings beats, 'sticky' inflation data is leading to hawkish rate expectations and divergence in policy.

themes.name: Utilities Sector Weakness
themes.description: Utilities are lagging amid AI power narrative and energy shock volatility, with only 6% above their 5/20-day moving averages.

raw_perplexity.text:
### 1. Mideast Conflict Energy Shock
**Why hot:** Middle East escalation driving Brent crude to $112–$119/bbl (up 5% last week), testing central bank inflation control; S&P 500 down 2% for fifth straight week (worst month in a year); unlocking thematic investments in energy security/infrastructure amid supply fears. Markets pricing in prolonged drag, flipping Fed cut expectations to hikes.  
**Keyword cues:** "Brent $112", "Mideast oil shock", "energy security", "supply chain resilience".  
**Example tickers:** XOM, CVX, SLB.  
**Sources:** [1], [5].

### 2. AI Power Demand Surge
**Why hot:** AI hyperscaler capex hit $131B in Q1 (67% YoY growth, beating consensus); colliding with Mideast energy shock to accelerate electrification/infrastructure buildout; BlackRock favoring active thematic exposures in public/private markets.  
**Keyword cues:** "AI power demand", "hyperscaler capex $131B", "electrification themes", "datacenter energy".  
**Example tickers:** NEE, VST, GEV.  
**Sources:** [1], [3].

### 3. Broad S&P Earnings Beats
**Why hot:** 74% EPS beats/78% sales beats (best since 2021); median stock +11% YoY growth (highest in 5 years); up/down guidance ratio above average, driven by Big Tech but broad-based. Earnings carrying rally amid geopolitics.  
**Keyword cues:** "74% EPS beats", "11% median earnings growth", "Q1 2026 beats".  
**Example tickers:** NVDA, MSFT, AAPL.  
**Sources:** [2], [3].

### 4. Medicaid Managed Care Turnaround
**Why hot:** Analyst upgrades after margins bottom in 2026; enrollment down 19% from COVID peak, risk pool stabilizing, rates adjusting higher; historical rallies happen in year one of visibility. Beat rates accelerating.  
**Keyword cues:** "Medicaid margins bottoming", "19% enrollment drop", "managed care upgrades".  
**Example tickers:** UNH, CNC, MOH.  
**Sources:** [2].

### 5. Tokenized Credit & Crypto Yield Plays
**Why hot:** Crypto market pullback shifting demand to tokenized credit products (home equity lines, private/public credit blends) for yield; new launches like US commercial paper on Solana (Galaxy Digital/Franklin Templeton/Coinbase); blending TradFi/crypto.  
**Keyword cues:** "tokenized commercial paper Solana", "private credit tokenization", "crypto yield products".  
**Example tickers:** COIN, MSTR, BLK.  
**Sources:** [4].

### 6. Mag 7 / Tech Positioning Shift
**Why hot:** Rotation from max bullish Mag 7 (late Oct peak) to caution; tech leading earnings but broader market jitters from conflict/volatility; key watch for next weeks' positioning unwind.  
**Keyword cues:** "Mag 7 caution", "tech positioning shift", "late October tech peak".  
**Example tickers:** NVDA, TSLA, AMZN.  
**Sources:** [4], [3].

### 7. Sticky Inflation & Hawkish Rates
**Why hot:** "Sticky" inflation data despite beats; Fed/BOE/ECB on hold, US 10yr yields to 4.43%, Europe/UK pricing hikes; markets diverging on policy (US tempered vs. peers hawkish).  
**Keyword cues:** "sticky inflation", "10yr 4.43%", "Fed hike expectations".  
**Example tickers:** TLT (short), GLD, TIP.  
**Sources:** [3], [5].

### 8. Utilities Sector Weakness
**Why hot:** Only 6% above 5/20-day MAs (vs. tech's 67–73%); potential rotation target amid AI power narrative, but lagging in energy shock volatility.  
**Keyword cues:** "utilities 6% above MA", "sector laggard energy shock".  
**Example tickers:** NEE, SO, DUK.  
**Sources:** [6].
